text:
Olympic800m champion Keely Hodgkinson was forced to swap her trainers for those of communications officer Liz Birchall before stepping onto the Stade de France podium to collect her gold medal.

Hodgkinson - who became Team GB’s first gold medalist in the 800m since Kelly Holmes in 2004 - is a Nike-sponsored athlete and claimed her second Olympic medal while wearing the Nike Zoom Victory 2 super shoe.

Team GB, however, are kitted out by Adidas with all of the athletes representing Great Britain required to wear the correct team kit on the podiums. This led to an awkward moment for Hodgkinson as she realised that she was still wearing her Nikes just moments before climbing onto the rostrum.

A member of the Team GB staff called on Adidas to secure a pair for Hodgkinson but had no luck, leaving Birchall to step in and save the day, handing over her pair. “I got my medal wearing Liz Birchall’s shoes,” the Olympic champion explained. “They did at least fit me.”

That mishap will be the last of Hodgkinson’s concerns after making history and delivering on expectations to claim one of 12 Olympic golds won by Team GB in Paris so far this summer as they battle with Australia and France for third place in the medal table.

Despite heading into the final of the 800m as the clear favourite with Team USA titan Athing Mu absent from the Games altogether, Hodgkinson didn’t have it all her own way. She was pushed all the way by Kenya’s Mary Moraa, who hung onto the shoulder of the Brit heading onto the second and final lap.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

“She wasn’t coming past me,” Hodgkinson stated. “You have to work out in the 800m that if you go too soon, you pay for it later. Mary really pushed me on the back straight. If I was in her position I’d have done the same.

“But she was on the outside and if you slowly wind it up, control your rhythm and energy, you still have something left in that last 100m. I’ve always had that finish, natural speed.

“But we’ve maximised it. My technique has changed. I’m a lot stronger in my core. It’s down to fine margins, but I would now back myself in a home-straight kick with Mary.”

Hodgkinson’s moment in the spotlight wasn’t replicated by Team GB’s other major gold hopeful, Josh Kerr. The Scot punched in a British record time and outran race favourite and arch-rival Jakob Ingebrigtsen in the 1500m final, but was unable to hold off a tremendous late kick from Cole Hocker of the USA.

title: ​Team GB forced to change trainers when receiving medal at Olympics | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Harry Smith
description: One Team GB star found herself in an awkward situation just moments before collecting an Olympic medal.
